content.problemStatement:
Chef has a sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã. This sequence has exactly 2N2^N2N subsequences. Chef considers a subsequence of AAA interesting if its size is exactly KKK and the sum of all its elements is minimum possible, i.e. there is no subsequence with size KKK which has a smaller sum.

Help Chef find the number of interesting subsequences of the sequence AAA.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the number of interesting subsequences.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: There are six subsequences with length 222: (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2), (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3), (1,4)(1, 4)(1,4), (2,3)(2, 3)(2,3), (2,4)(2, 4)(2,4) and (3,4)(3, 4)(3,4). The minimum sum is 333 and the only subsequence with this sum is (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2).
